_readme: Copy this file, rename it to your-company-slug.json, fill in the fields, and delete all '_comment' keys. The file will auto-render on the experience page sorted by period (most recent first).
_comment_id: Unique kebab-case slug. Must match the filename (minus .json). Also used by project JSONs to link projects to this experience — keep it short and stable.
_comment_company: Full legal or well-known name. The projects page trims anything in parentheses for compact badge display.
_comment_period: Format: 'Mon YYYY – Mon YYYY' or 'Mon YYYY – Present'. Used for sorting (most recent first). Month abbreviations: Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep Oct Nov Dec
_comment_tags: 3-5 short topic tags shown as pills on the card. Summarise the domains you worked in. Keep them consistent with other experiences where possible.
_comment_metrics: 2-3 headline numbers shown on the card. Value can be any string (%, $, ×, raw number). Label should be ≤ 15 characters.
highlights: One-line area of focus or achievement
_comment_highlights: 3-4 bullet fragments shown on the card as a quick preview. These are NOT the full detail bullets — keep them short (≤ 8 words each).
_comment_technologies: Full list of tools and technologies used. First 3 appear as badges on the card; the rest appear in the detail panel. Order by importance / how much you used them.
details: Led X initiative that resulted in Y outcome — quantify wherever possible.
Designed and implemented Z system to solve A problem.
Automated B process using C tools, reducing manual effort by D%.
Collaborated with E team to deliver F feature within G timeline.
Mentored / enabled team on H technology or practice.
_comment_details: Full bullet points shown in the detail panel. Each string is one bullet. Aim for 5-10 bullets. Start with a strong action verb (Led, Built, Designed, Automated, Implemented, Drove). Quantify outcomes where possible.
